scenarios.title: One shared current-document capability owns every credential surface

gherkin.given: every registered Research Lab page is available
gherkin.when: the index and every tool source and live page are inspected
gherkin.then: no page defines a tool-local editor getter setter writer store or request broker, index exposes status and erase-only cleanup only, and any approved collection and use stay in one loaded document

linkedTests.testId: Canary BUG-001: real index loads shared status and erase controls with no credential editor
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: The real index loads RLDATA before RLAPP and exposes non-secret status plus erase-only cleanup with zero current credential input.

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: one shared current-document capability owns every credential surface
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: Registry-derived source and live-page inspection finds one shared owner, no tool-local credential API, and no current index credential input.

scenarios.title: Every lifecycle and document boundary starts unconfigured

gherkin.given: a controlled eligible provider is configured in the current loaded document
gherkin.when: the user reloads, changes route, navigates pages, traverses bfcache, closes or reopens, or opens another tab window or context
gherkin.then: every affected document is unconfigured and no browser bridge transfers the credential

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: every lifecycle and document boundary starts unconfigured
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: Reload, route change, bfcache traversal, HTML-page navigation, close or reopen, tab, window, iframe, and browser-context boundaries all begin unconfigured and carry no bridge payload.

scenarios.title: Same-document use reveals status and sanitized results, never the credential

gherkin.given: one provider policy authorizes one operation in the current loaded document
gherkin.when: the user enters a sentinel credential and invokes that operation without leaving the document
gherkin.then: the shared field is blanked immediately, only status and a sanitized result are visible, and no caller receives the credential headers raw response or transferable credential object

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: same-document use blanks input and exposes status but never the credential
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: The controlled eligible document owns input and request, blanks the field immediately, exposes sanitized output only, and clears at the next lifecycle boundary.

scenarios.title: Legacy detection is metadata-only and cleanup is erase-only

gherkin.given: known registered legacy containers exist and the current-document runtime is empty
gherkin.when: name-only detection runs and the user dismisses or confirms destructive whole-container deletion
gherkin.then: detection reports provider and location metadata plus counts only, dismissal performs no read or mutation, confirmed cleanup verifies registered names absent, and the runtime remains unconfigured

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: legacy detection is metadata-only and cleanup is erase-only
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: The UI exposes redacted metadata only, destructive disclosure precedes confirmation, dismissal is inert, whole registered containers are deleted without value access, and success requires every selected name absent.

gherkin.given: the current-document runtime has a known baseline state
gherkin.when: a caller supplies unknown empty constructor or __proto__ provider identifiers
gherkin.then: the call fails safely and runtime policy storage surfaces and prototypes remain unchanged

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: unknown and prototype-shaped providers fail without mutation
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: Unknown, empty, inherited, constructor, and prototype-shaped IDs fail closed without changing runtime, policy, storage surfaces, or prototypes.

scenarios.title: Clear-all removes current memory first and erases whole legacy containers

gherkin.given: the current document holds an eligible credential and known legacy containers exist
gherkin.then: current memory is cleared first, registered containers are erased without value readback, and incomplete erasure remains explicit without restoring state

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: clear all empties current memory before whole-container erase
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: Clear all drops current memory first, removes whole registered containers without value readback, and reports explicit incomplete cleanup without restoring state.

scenarios.title: A sentinel credential leaves no rendered or diagnostic trace

gherkin.given: a unique sentinel credential is configured
gherkin.when: rendering failures clear lifecycle boundaries and disclosure scans are exercised
gherkin.then: the sentinel is absent from browser output diagnostics URLs referrers storage bridges and test artifacts

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: sentinel credential never appears in DOM console errors URL or referrer
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: The sentinel is absent from DOM, accessibility output, events, console, errors, analytics, URLs, referrers, browser bridges, screenshots, traces, snapshots, and test artifacts.

gherkin.given: index and every registered tool are separate HTML documents
gherkin.when: the user follows a tool link after viewing provider status or legacy cleanup on index and every registered source and live page is inspected
gherkin.then: every tool starts unconfigured with no editor getter setter writer broker store or browser transfer bridge and index makes no continuity claim

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: index-to-tool navigation transfers no credential and every tool starts unconfigured
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: Index makes no continuity claim; every registered tool starts empty and receives zero credential data through URL, message, worker, cookie, opener, storage, history, DOM, or equivalent bridges.

scenarios.title: Twelve Data stays disabled without authorization evidence

gherkin.given: no approved Twelve Data browser authorization record exists
gherkin.when: a tool requests a credential-backed Twelve Data fetch
gherkin.then: no request is sent and a safe provider-disabled status appears

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: Twelve Data browser credential calls remain disabled without authorization evidence
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: Twelve Data is rejected before credential collection and sends zero browser credential requests or alternate fallbacks.

scenarios.title: Header authentication never becomes query authentication

gherkin.given: a controlled policy verifies browser use exact operation origin same-document ownership and header authentication
gherkin.when: the request is built sent and receives an auth failure
gherkin.then: the credential stays only in the approved header and no query proxy origin provider or transport fallback occurs

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: approved header auth never places credentials in URLs or retries with query auth
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: A controlled eligible policy performs one exact-origin approved-header attempt with no credential URL, query, redirect, referrer, provider, origin, proxy, or transport fallback.

scenarios.title: G028 closure preserves rlData, collision state, dirty hunks, and canaries

gherkin.given: the nine-row G028 ledger the blind spot the rlData contract F004-COLLISION-001 and the protected dirty tree
gherkin.when: memory-only remediation scanner disposition and cross-feature canaries are assessed
gherkin.then: every finding has one disposition, no persisted credential remains, rlData and dirty hunks survive, and provider Bond Causal and FX canaries retain their contracts

linkedTests.testId: Regression BUG-001: G028 inventory closes genuine rows without deleting noncredential rlData cache
linkedTests.requiredAssertion: Every G028 row and the central-store blind spot receive one disposition while non-secret rlData, Feature 004 protected hunks, collision evidence, and framework immutability remain intact.
